payload.project_name: B82 Southern Brooklyn Select Bus Service
payload.description: Implementation of Select Bus Service on the B82 corridor in Southern Brooklyn, including dedicated bus lanes, transit signal priority, off-board fare collection, all-door boarding, and bus stop improvements. Launch planned for July 2018.

evidence_refs.source_quote: July 2018: Launch B82 Southern Brooklyn Select Bus Service

tool_args.label: Southern Brooklyn B82 Corridor
tool_args.raw_text: The Southern Brooklyn B82 corridor was identified as a potential candidate for Select Bus Service in the 2009 Bus Rapid Transit Phase II Study. Serves 28,000 daily riders on B82 Limited and Local.

payload.limits: Cropsey Ave & Bay 37 St to Flatlands Ave / Kings Highway / Rockaway Pkwy
payload.description: The B82 corridor identified as a potential candidate for Select Bus Service in the 2009 Bus Rapid Transit Phase II Study. Serves 28,000 daily riders.

tool_args.label: Kings Highway (Southern Brooklyn)
tool_args.raw_text: Wide, boulevard style portion of Kings Highway. Major east-west transit corridor – Used by B82 Local, B82 Limited, B7 Local, school buses, coach buses, other buses – During AM and PM rush hours, buses come once per minute along Kings Hwy.

tool_args.label: Dedicated Bus Lanes
tool_args.raw_text: Dedicated Bus Lanes. Bus lane one-way, along curb. Bus lanes serve all buses, emergency vehicles, and right turns. Bus lanes in effect 7-10 AM & 4-7 PM Mon-Fri. Parking is allowed in bus lanes at all other times.

payload.description: Bus lane one-way, along curb. Bus lanes serve all buses, emergency vehicles, and right turns. In effect 7-10 AM & 4-7 PM Mon-Fri. Parking allowed in bus lanes at all other times.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Bus lanes serve all buses, emergency vehicles, and right turns
Bus lanes in effect 7-10 AM & 4-7 PM Mon-Fri
Parking is allowed in bus lanes at all other times

tool_args.raw_text: Transit Signal Priority – keeps lights green longer for buses (& traffic)

payload.description: Transit Signal Priority – keeps lights green longer for buses (& traffic)

tool_args.label: Off-Board Fare Collection
tool_args.raw_text: Off-Board Fare Collection. Real-time passenger information screens and off-board fare payment.

payload.description: Off-board fare payment at SBS stops with fare machines

tool_args.label: All-Door Boarding
tool_args.raw_text: All-Door Boarding. Quicker bus boarding. Buses spend less time waiting at bus stops.

payload.description: All-door boarding for quicker bus boarding, buses spend less time waiting at bus stops

tool_args.label: Improved Bus Stop Amenities
tool_args.raw_text: Larger, lighted bus shelters. Reconstructed, level landing platforms. Wider medians with ample space for passengers. Added seating. ADA accessible bus stops with subway-style edge strips.

payload.description: Larger, lighted bus shelters, added seating, reconstructed level landing platforms, ADA accessible bus stops with subway-style edge strips, wider medians with ample space for passengers

tool_args.label: Bus Lane Segments in CB 15
tool_args.raw_text: 1. McDonald Ave to E 4 St (westbound) 2. Ocean Pkwy to Cl Ave (westbound) 3. E 13 St to E 15 St (eastbound) 4. E 16 St to E 18 St (westbound) 5. E 18 St to Ocean Ave (eastbound)

payload.description: Five bus lane segments in CB 15: 1. McDonald Ave to E 4 St (westbound), 2. Ocean Pkwy to Cl Ave (westbound), 3. E 13 St to E 15 St (eastbound), 4. E 16 St to E 18 St (westbound), 5. E 18 St to Ocean Ave (eastbound)

tool_args.label: Kings Highway Median Reconstruction (2017)
tool_args.raw_text: Kings Hwy/E 27 St: Reconstructed B82/B7 bus stop median on southwest corner to wider, ADA-accessible median. Kings Hwy/Nostrand Ave: Reconstructed two B82/B7 bus stop medians to wider, ADA-accessible medians. Added larger shelter and began preparations for other amenities to be added in Spring 2018.

payload.description: Reconstructed B82/B7 bus stop median at Kings Hwy/E 27 St to wider, ADA-accessible median. Reconstructed two B82/B7 bus stop medians at Kings Hwy/Nostrand Ave to wider, ADA-accessible medians. Added larger shelter and began preparations for other amenities to be added in Spring 2018.

tool_args.label: B82 Delay Type Breakdown
tool_args.raw_text: Delay Type Percentage In Motion 48% Signal Delay 29% Bus Stops 21% Other Delays 2%. Buses are delayed by traffic, double parking, difficulty pulling in and out of bus stops.

payload.claim_text: Buses are delayed by traffic, double parking, difficulty pulling in and out of bus stops. In Motion 48%, Signal Delay 29%, Bus Stops 21%, Other Delays 2%

warnings: claim.route is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Route ids should link to route records when supported. Suggested relation: route has_claim claim when the route endpoint resolves exactly.

tool_args.label: Kings Highway Business Survey Results
tool_args.raw_text: Interviews with 235 businesses along Kings Highway. 67% of businesses report double parking interferes with deliveries. 46% of businesses report vehicles at curb interfere with deliveries. 2/3 of deliveries take 15 minutes or less. 48% of businesses would find a delivery zone helpful.

payload.claim_text: Interviews with 235 businesses along Kings Highway. 67% of businesses report double parking interferes with deliveries. 46% report vehicles at curb interfere with deliveries. 2/3 of deliveries take 15 minutes or less. 48% of businesses would find a delivery zone helpful.

tool_args.label: Kings Highway Parking Space Changes
tool_args.raw_text: # of Parking Spaces. Part-time spaces used for bus lanes: -108. 7-10 AM, 4-7 PM Mon-Fri. Full-time spaces removed: -4 (bus stop lengthening at 2 stops). New full-time spaces created: +21 (Bus stop removal, new angled/parallel parking). Part-time parking changes for bus lanes only apply for 25% of the day on weekdays. For 75% of the day on weekdays and full-time on weekends, there will be more parking available than there is currently.

payload.claim_text: Part-time spaces used for bus lanes: -108 (7-10 AM, 4-7 PM Mon-Fri). Full-time spaces removed: -4 (bus stop lengthening at 2 stops). New full-time spaces created: +21 (Bus stop removal, new angled/parallel parking). Part-time parking changes for bus lanes only apply for 25% of the day on weekdays. For 75% of the day on weekdays and full-time on weekends, there will be more parking available than there is currently.

tool_args.label: Slow Bus Speeds Along Kings Highway
tool_args.raw_text: Traffic/parking/truck loading lead to slow bus speeds along Kings Highway. Routing from Flatlands Ave to Kings Hwy is slow, adds to congestion. Complicated routing around Rockaway Pkwy Station leads to slow bus speeds.

payload.claim_text: Traffic/parking/truck loading lead to slow bus speeds along Kings Highway. Routing from Flatlands Ave to Kings Hwy is slow, adds to congestion. Complicated routing around Rockaway Pkwy Station leads to slow bus speeds.

warnings: claim.route is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Route ids should link to route records when supported. Suggested relation: route has_claim claim when the route endpoint resolves exactly.

tool_args.label: Key Safety Issues on B82 in CB 15
tool_args.raw_text: Key Safety Issues Identified along the B82 Route in CB 15: Long pedestrian crossings and lack of pedestrian refuges to cross Kings Hwy. Narrow median bus stops along Kings Hwy. Limited visibility at some intersections.

payload.claim_text: Key safety issues identified along the B82 Route in CB 15: Long pedestrian crossings and lack of pedestrian refuges to cross Kings Hwy, narrow median bus stops along Kings Hwy, limited visibility at some intersections.

warnings: claim.route is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Route ids should link to route records when supported. Suggested relation: route has_claim claim when the route endpoint resolves exactly.

tool_args.label: Kings Highway Shopper Survey Mode Split
tool_args.raw_text: Face-to-face interviews with 7,500 people on Kings Highway between W 10th St and Ocean Ave. Weekday Method: Walk 51%, Car 23%, Transit 24%, Bus 11%, Subway 13%. Weekend Method: Walk 58%, Car 21%, Transit 19%, Bus 8%, Subway 11%.

payload.claim_text: Face-to-face interviews with 7,500 people on Kings Highway between W 10th St and Ocean Ave. Weekday: Walk 51%, Car 23%, Transit 24% (Bus 11%, Subway 13%). Weekend: Walk 58%, Car 21%, Transit 19% (Bus 8%, Subway 11%).
